hints: It is a pocket-sized electronic device.
Almost everyone in modern society carries one.
It combines cellular access, web surfing, and touch applications.
It replaced traditional landlines and features digital cameras.
Popular brands include Apple iPhone and Samsung Galaxy.
It is an ancient astronomical instrument.
Used by astronomers and navigators to measure celestial altitude.
Commonly made of brass with circular plates called tympans.
It helps solve problems relating to time and the position of stars.
It was largely replaced by the sextant in the 18th century.
It is a historical scientific object.
Invented independently in 1745 by Ewald Georg von Kleist and Pieter van Musschenbroek.
It consists of a glass jar lined with metal foil on both the inside and outside.
It was the earliest form of electrical capacitor used to store high-voltage charges.
Benjamin Franklin used it in his famous electricity experiments.
This person is associated with modern physics.
He spent his later years researching at Princeton.
He won the 1921 Nobel Prize in Physics for the photoelectric effect.
He is famous for his formula E=mc².
He developed the special and general theories of relativity.
This historical figure is celebrated in computer history.
She was the daughter of the famous romantic poet Lord Byron.
She worked closely with inventor Charles Babbage.
She wrote notes on Babbage's Analytical Engine mechanical computer.
She is widely recognized as the world's first computer programmer.
He was an ancient Greek polymath.
He served as the chief librarian at the Library of Alexandria.
He invented the discipline of geography and the terminology still used today.
He created an algorithm for finding prime numbers by filtering multiples.
He calculated the Earth's circumference using shadows cast by obelisks.
It is a large predatory feline.
Native primarily to sub-Saharan Africa.
Known for living in family groups called prides.
Males grow large dark manes around their heads.
Often referred to as the 'King of the Jungle'.
It is a semi-aquatic mammal.
Endemic to eastern Australia, including Tasmania.
It is one of the few species of egg-laying mammals.
It has a duck-like bill, beaver-like tail, and otter-like feet.
Males possess venomous spurs on their hind feet.
It is an aquatic amphibian.
Endemic exclusively to Lake Xochimilco in Mexico.
It exhibits neoteny, keeping its larval gills into adulthood.
Highly researched for its ability to regenerate entire limbs.
Also known as the Mexican walking fish.
It is a global communications system.
Evolved from ARPANET in the late 20th century.
Enables access to the World Wide Web and email.
Uses IP addresses and packet switching routing.
The largest interconnected network of computers worldwide.
It is a digital bookkeeping technology.
Information is stored chronologically in connected segments.
Relies on consensus mechanisms like Proof of Work or Proof of Stake.
Forms the cryptographic backbone of decentralized virtual tokens.
Invented in 2008 for cryptocurrency, specifically Bitcoin.
It is a fundamental electrical component.
The name combines 'memory' and 'resistor'.
Its resistance depends on the history of electric current that has passed through it.
Theorized by Leon Chua in 1971, with first physical models built in 2008 by HP Labs.
Considered the fourth passive circuit element alongside resistor, capacitor, and inductor.
